text: The latest update did not improve battery life at all.
rationale: Tests whether the model picks up the negation cue or just the trigger word 'improve'

text: This app is no longer reliable on my phone.
rationale: Strong positive cue 'reliable' negated by 'no longer'; tests cue-vs-context handling

text: The team won the championship final last weekend.
rationale: Sports content unrelated to app-review training; tests domain-shift robustness

text: The update didn't exactly make the app any less confusing.
rationale: Tests if the model is fooled by 'didn't' and 'less' into thinking the sentiment is positive.

text: I can't say that I'm not disappointed with the new UI.
rationale: Uses double negation ('can't say' + 'not disappointed') to see if the model picks up the underlying negative sentiment.

text: The performance never fails to be anything but mediocre.
rationale: Uses 'never fails' followed by a negative descriptor to test complex negation handling.

text: This was a masterclass in how to build a terrible interface.
rationale: Contains the positive trigger 'masterclass' in a context that is fundamentally negative.

text: While the support was excellent, the product itself is unusable.
rationale: Tests if the strong positive 'excellent' outweighs the final 'unusable' prediction.

text: It’s a perfect example of a complete disaster.
rationale: Uses 'perfect' as a trigger to see if the model ignores the negative noun 'disaster'.

text: The team played a 4-4-2 formation and focused on long balls.
rationale: Tests the model on sports commentary which has no inherent app-review sentiment.

text: Add two tablespoons of olive oil to the pan and heat slowly.
rationale: Tests if a cooking recipe is correctly classified as neutral/no-sentiment.

text: The central bank decided to keep interest rates unchanged this quarter.
rationale: Tests financial news prose to see if the model stays neutral outside its training domain.

text: Fix it.
rationale: Extremely short command to see if the model can infer sentiment from two words.

text: The app works but the UI is old and the buttons are small and the colors are ugly and the font is hard to read and it crashes often and I hate it.
rationale: A long, repetitive sentence to see if the model maintains focus on the sentiment until the end.

text: Wow.
rationale: Single word exclamation to test if the model can handle minimal input.

text: Great, another bug to spend my entire weekend fixing.
rationale: Uses 'Great' sarcastically to see if the model detects the frustration.

text: I really love waiting ten minutes for the login screen to load.
rationale: The word 'love' is used to describe a negative experience (long wait times).

text: Thanks for deleting all my data with the last update, very helpful.
rationale: Uses 'helpful' sarcastically to describe a catastrophic failure.

text: It is not that the app is bad, it is just not good.
rationale: Tests the nuance between 'not bad' and 'not good'.

text: The only thing reliable about this app is how consistently it crashes.
rationale: Uses 'reliable' to describe a negative behavior (crashing).

text: The photosynthesis process converts light energy into chemical energy.
rationale: Scientific fact to check for domain-shift handling.

text: A masterpiece of engineering that simplifies every complex task I face daily.
rationale: Testing a long, purely positive sentence to ensure no 'length' bias.

text: The UI is so intuitive that I only needed a 50-page manual to find the exit button.
rationale: Sarcastic use of 'intuitive' to describe a confusing interface.

text: I used to love this app, but now I find it quite frustrating.
rationale: Testing 'Temporal Shift' (Past vs. Present) to see if the model recognizes the current sentiment.

text: The interface is okay, but the features are lacking.
rationale: Testing 'Comparative Sentiment' where one part is neutral and the other is negative.

text: This is the best worst app I have ever used.
rationale: Oxymoron test ('best worst') to see which word the model prioritizes.

text: No one should ever avoid using this amazing tool.
rationale: Uses 'No one' and 'avoid' (double negative) to praise the tool.

text: The stability is an absolute joke.
rationale: Positive trigger 'absolute' used to emphasize a negative noun 'joke'.

text: The movie's plot was predictable and the acting was subpar.
rationale: Testing sentiment in a different domain (film review) to see if it generalizes.

text: Okay.
rationale: Neutral short response.

text: Oh, I'm sure the developers worked really 'hard' on this broken feature.
rationale: Uses quotation marks and sarcasm to imply lack of effort.

text: The app did not disappoint at all.
rationale: Tests if 'did not' + 'disappoint' is correctly seen as positive.

text: A total failure of an otherwise brilliant concept.
rationale: Positive trigger 'brilliant' vs. negative 'failure'.
